county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Coke County formed 1889 from Tom Green. County seat is Robert Lee. Central Texas region with farming, ranching, and diverse immigrant heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Robert Lee. West Texas county. Fort Chadbourne (frontier military post) — records at NARA. Check Tom Green County for pre-1889 records. Ranching heritage. Robert Lee courthouse.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Coke County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1889; County Clerk in Robert Lee holds deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Coke County Deed Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1889 to present. Continuous coverage from county formation.

source_profiles.source_name: Coke County Marriage Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses from 1889. Texas county clerks have maintained marriage records since county formation. State registration from 1966.

source_profiles.source_name: Coke County Probate Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Probate records, wills, estate inventories, and guardianships from 1889. Texas probate records are maintained at the county level by the County Clerk.

source_profiles.source_name: Coke County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1889 to 1950 for Coke County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.source_name: Texas General Land Office -- Coke County Land Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: State land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Coke County from 1889. GLO archives include searchable online databases.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Coleman County formed 1858 from Brown, Travis. County seat is Coleman. Central Texas region with farming, ranching, and diverse immigrant heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Coleman. Central-west Texas county. Camp Colorado (frontier military post). Coleman County Historical Museum. Check Brown and Travis for pre-1858 records. Ranching and farming heritage.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Coleman County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1858; County Clerk in Coleman holds deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Collin County formed 1846 from Fannin. County seat is McKinney. North Texas / Blackland Prairie region with farming and urban growth heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in McKinney. North Texas county, now heavily suburban Dallas area. Collin County Historical Society. McKinney Performing Arts Center archives. Freedmen's communities (Colony, near Plano). Check Fannin County for pre-1846 records. Peter's Colony records at TSLAC.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Collin County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1846; County Clerk in McKinney holds deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Collin County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1850 to 1950 for Collin County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Collingsworth County formed 1876 from Bexar, Fannin. County seat is Wellington. Texas Panhandle / South Plains region with ranching and farming heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Wellington. Panhandle county organized 1890. Ranching heritage. Collingsworth County Museum. Check Bexar and Fannin for pre-organization records. Rolling Plains geography.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Collingsworth County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1876; County Clerk in Wellington holds deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Colorado County formed 1837 from Original Texas county (Municipality of Colorado). County seat is Columbus. Gulf Coast region with port, plantation, and petrochemical heritage. One of the original Texas counties from the Republic of Texas era.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Columbus. Original Texas county — one of the Old Three Hundred colony areas. Colorado County Historical Commission. Stafford Opera House Museum. Significant German and Czech immigrant communities. Strong plantation economy — enslaved persons records. Columbus has well-preserved historic architecture.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Colorado County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1837; County Clerk in Columbus holds deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Colorado County Deed Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1837 to present. Continuous coverage from county formation.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Probate records, wills, estate inventories, and guardianships from 1837. Texas probate records are maintained at the county level by the County Clerk.

source_profiles.source_name: Colorado County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1850 to 1950 for Colorado County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.source_name: Colorado County Enslaved Persons Records -- TSLAC / County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Estate inventories listing enslaved persons, bills of sale, tax rolls listing slaveholders and enslaved persons counts, and Freedmen's Bureau records (NARA). Colorado County was part of the Texas plantation economy.

source_profiles.source_name: Colorado County German Church Records (Lutheran, Catholic, Methodist)
source_profiles.repository: Local congregations / TSLAC / Texas Lutheran University archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: German immigrant church records including baptisms, confirmations, marriages, and burials. Colorado County has significant German settlement heritage. German-language records may require translation. Check local Lutheran, Catholic, and Methodist congregations.

source_profiles.source_name: Texas General Land Office -- Colorado County Land Grants
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Republic of Texas and early statehood land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Colorado County. GLO archives are online with searchable databases. Texas managed its own public lands (unique among states). Empresario colony records if applicable.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Comal County formed 1846 from Bexar, Gonzales, Travis. County seat is New Braunfels. Texas Hill Country region with German, Czech, and frontier heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in New Braunfels. Hill Country county — founded by German immigrants under Prince Carl of Solms-Braunfels (1845). Sophienburg Museum and Archives in New Braunfels has extensive German immigration records. New Braunfels Herald-Zeitung (German-language newspaper archives). Lutheran and Catholic church records. Comal County is one of the best-documented German settlements in Texas.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Comal County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1846; County Clerk in New Braunfels holds deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Cooke County formed 1848 from Fannin. County seat is Gainesville. North Texas / Blackland Prairie region with farming and urban growth heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Gainesville. North Texas county. Great Hanging at Gainesville (1862) — 42 Unionists executed during Civil War, documented in court records. Morton Museum in Gainesville. Cooke County Heritage Society. Check Fannin for pre-1848 records. Chisholm Trail passed through Cooke County.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Cooke County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1848; County Clerk in Gainesville holds deed books
